heading: Stop a Stream Processor
body:
> **Note:** Atlas Stream Processing discards the internal state of stream processors which
> have been `stopped` for 45 days or more. When you start such a
> processor, it operates and reports [statistics](atlas-sp-manage-processor-stats) identically to its initial run.To stop a stream processor:**Admin-api:**Atlas Administration APIThe Atlas Administration API provides an endpoint for
stopping a stream processor.[Stop One Stream Processor](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-stopgroupstreamprocessor)**Atlas UI:**Atlas UITo stop a stream processor in the Atlas UI, go to the
**Stream Processing** page for your Atlas project
and click **Manage** in the pane for your stream processing workspace to
view the list of stream processors defined for it.Then, click the **Stop** icon for your stream processor.**mongosh:**`mongosh`To stop an existing stream processor with [mongosh](mongodb-shell), use the
[sp.processor.stop()](manual/reference/method/sp.processor.stop) method. It has the following syntax:```sh
sp.processor.stop(<options>)
```Where `<options>` is a generic, optional document whose fields are
passed to the underlying stop command.For example, to stop a stream processor named `proc01`, run the
following command:**Input:**```shell
sp.proc01.stop()
```**Output:**```shell
{ "ok" : 1 }
```This method returns `{ "ok": 1 }` if the stream processor exists
and is currently running. If you invoke `sp.processor.stop()`
for a stream processor that is not `running`, [mongosh](mongodb-shell) will
return an error.
